macro_summary: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +57k (cooling from +129k); unemployment 4.2% (down); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). US equities edged higher Monday as tech led a rebound from last week's selloff—QQQ rose 1.2% and semis rallied on AI demand optimism and a Waller-driven rate-hike repricing that pushed the 6-month T-bill yield to 4%. Oil held near $69, erasing the Hormuz war premium as supplies normalized, while US-Iran de-escalation talks remained in focus.
commodities_note: WTI at $68.82, up 0.6% on the day but down 28% over the month—pre-war levels—as OPEC+ output rises and stranded tankers exit the Gulf. Gold slipped 0.4% to $4,158.50, and the US dollar was nearly flat. No benchmark data anomaly reported.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks are hard-blocked on technical extension after multi-week rallies, with RSI readings above 70 across the board. NIM compression remains the structural headwind — US10Y at 4.48% is above the 4.20% relief threshold, and MAS S$NEER tightening won't ease until October. Divergence is minimal; DBS and OCBC are equally overbought. No action at current prices.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — waiting for NIM relief signals
singapore.key_development: The US10Y held above 4.20% again Monday, keeping the dual NIM-compression channels (USD funding costs and strong SGD) firmly intact for the July quarter.

semiconductors.summary: Semis cluster is deeply split: AMD, MU, and ASML are extended beyond 10% above their 50-day, while NVDA, AVGO, and LITE are all 6-18% below. Monday's SOXX +2.2% was driven by a rebound in the beaten-down names (AVGO +3.4% on Apple chip deal) and a continuation of the memory/HBM trade (MU +0.8%). SK Hynix's $28B Nasdaq IPO and Samsung's earnings (this week) will be the decisive catalysts for memory allocation; ASML and TSM earnings next week add binary risk. Wait for post-print setups.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — bifurcated risk/reward
semiconductors.key_development: SK Hynix trimmed its Nasdaq IPO target to $28B (per WSJ), while Samsung is expected to report $55B operating profit this week — both will clarify HBM demand durability.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is cautious: MSFT is now 10% below its 52-week high, facing a 400-newspaper lawsuit and Azure OpenAI concentration concerns. AMZN and GOOG are near support but lack catalysts. PLTR is momentum-blocked after a 17% 5-day surge. The cluster's structural AI capex story is intact (Meta, $200B AWS, TPU ramp), but near-term sentiment is fragile. Look for a relief rally if MSFT's July 29 earnings show non-OpenAI Azure RPO growth above 30%.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but credit-circularity and legal risks persist
big_tech.key_development: Xbox job cuts (3,200 layoffs, per Yahoo Finance) and the 400-newspaper AI lawsuit deepened MSFT's risk premium; meanwhile, Amazon's forward-deployed engineer push mirrors Palantir's playbook.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier after SK Hynix and Micron — Samsung's earnings this week will signal whether HBM supply diversity improves, directly impacting MU and 000660.KS pricing power.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung's Q2 earnings (this week) confirm HBM3E qualification with NVDA and gross margin expansion above 20%.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA invested $2B alongside Lumentum for AI optics — Coherent is the S&P 500 peer and a direct competitor in co-packaged optics, with the same structural demand driver as LITE.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after LITE's next earnings (Aug 12) for photonics order book comparison.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and networking silicon for hyperscalers — directly benefits from AVGO's ASIC dominance and NVDA's networking demand, with exposure to the same capex cycle.
interconnected.entry_note: Add if NVDA's Blackwell Ultra ramp commentary signals custom ASIC pullback, which would benefit Marvell's networking share.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Leading high-speed data-center switch vendor for AI clusters — tied to MSFT, GOOG, and AMZN capex; a beat on hyperscaler capex guidance would directly lift Arista's revenue outlook.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch if MSFT/GOOG capex guides beat expectations, as Arista is a key backend networking supplier.

active_risks: Fed's Waller flagged high inflation as the chief risk, putting a July rate hike on the table (per Reuters, Jul 6) — hawkish pressure resurfaces despite the June payrolls miss.
USMCA not extended by Trump, moving to annual reviews — adds trade uncertainty for North American supply chains and agricultural exports (per Food Business News, Jul 6).
Iran-Houthi envoy reappears in Sana'a, signaling Tehran-backed escalation in Yemen (per Jerusalem Post, Jul 6) — low immediate market impact but keeps geopolitical risk premium alive.
Ukraine struck Russian airbase in Crimea and multiple ammunition depots (per Euromaidan Press, Jul 5) — grinding conflict with limited direct market effect.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil's collapse to $69 has removed the energy premium that previously pressured semis and consumer stocks; tech can rally without the headwind. SG banks' NIM compression persists as US10Y holds above 4.20%. The Fed's hawkish lean via Waller is capping multiple expansion, keeping valuations in check despite AI demand optimism.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Waller explicitly stated risks tilted toward high inflation and a rate hike is 'on the table' for July (per Reuters). USMCA annual-review phase began after Trump declined a 16-year extension; China and US agreed to reduce agricultural non-tariff barriers during the May Trump visit.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, WTI stays near $69; semis rebound continues but remains capped by extension and earnings risk. Fed holds rates in July, US10Y drifts near 4.5%. GOOG TPU ramp and MSFT Azure ex-OpenAI growth provide pockets of opportunity.

optimistic.description: US-Iran deal finalises, oil drops further to $65, and CPI cools to 4.0% — rate hike bets vanish. Semis multiples expand, NVDA reclaims $198, and ASML rallies through $1,865 pre-earnings. Singapore bank NIM relief triggers on US10Y break below 4.20%.

pessimistic.description: CPI prints above 4.5%, Waller hikes in July; DXY surges, semis sold off, and the SOXX retests May lows. ASML and TSM earnings disappoint; MU and AMD extension collapses. USMCA annual review intensifies tariff fears, hitting INTC and semiconductor trade.

wildcard.description: Hormuz closure due to Houthi escalation or a cyberattack on Saudi oil infrastructure; WTI spikes to $90+, gold surges as safe haven. NVDA and semis crash on energy shock, while BE and IFX surge as on-site power plays.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Stay defensive: with 8 of 29 names hard-blocked on extension and critical earnings ahead, new entries are limited. Wait for post-earnings setups in ASML/TSM and monitor Samsung/Hynix for memory cycle signals.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 8 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, leaving only a handful of actionable setups (NVDA near support, WRD oversold). The Fed's hawkish lean and rising 6M T-bill yields add macro headwinds.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: CPI eases to 4.0% or below (vs 4.3% May), reducing rate hike pressure and lifting growth stocks. Semis rally on multiples expansion.
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: CPI hot above 4.5%, rate hike odds surge above 50%. Semis sell off sharply, breaking NVDA support and extending CAUTION signals across the cluster.

first_pass_critical_violations: CBRS: signal=CAUTION writeup.what_to_do lacks a numeric re-entry zone (expected something like '$300–310' or 'SGD 21.19'). Per PRIORITY 0 §0.2, every CAUTION write-up under the depth mandate must name a price level.

AXTI: signal=CAUTION writeup.what_to_do lacks a numeric re-entry zone (expected something like '$300–310' or 'SGD 21.19'). Per PRIORITY 0 §0.2, every CAUTION write-up under the depth mandate must name a price level.

macro_context_line: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +57k (cooling from +129k); unemployment 4.2% (down); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). Reconcile the rate lean in the macro_summary against this when a CPI/FOMC/jobs event is near.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=3) / ACCUMULATE (n=5) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
